description:
Offered: every semester
This course explores, in depth, a specific topic area within psychological science. The topic areas will reflect the expertise and interests of the psychological science faculty. An emphasis is placed on reading primary literature and journal articles, and the course acquaints the student with methodology used in contemporary research in the specific area. Students are expected to complete or conduct a scientific literature review as part of this experience.
Prerequisite(s): PSY 2050 or PSY 2100 and Junior or Senior standing
Note: Course may be taken up to three times with different topics.
prerequisites: PSY 2050 or PSY 2100 and Junior or Senior standing
